verdict_reasoning: SRC is a canonical cytoplasmic non-receptor tyrosine kinase — its wild-type baseline compartment is cytoplasmic and inner-leaflet anchored via N-terminal myristoylation and palmitoylation. However, SRC has documented cell-state-induced surface display in oncogenic / transformed cells: tumor cells display SRC on the outer leaflet where it functions as an ecto-kinase, phosphorylating extracellular substrates and ligands. This cancer-cell ecto-pool is well-characterized in the literature and qualifies as a non-baseline surface context. Additionally, SRC is the target of multiple surface-directed therapeutic programs (antibodies, CAR-T, bispecifics) that engage the cancer-cell-surface SRC pool. The canonical baseline is intracellular; the cancer ecto-form is the surface story.
